the existing graph_test.ts/resolve_test.ts REQUIRED pivot case, restated as a golden case

G19 — the victim-phrase alias source exists in the Python resolver's lookup table but was never merged into web/data/alias-map.json at build time, so this phrase has NO exact key and falls to the fuzzy stage. It still lands on the right node (robb-is-killed) via the slug-token bonus, but matchType is "fuzzy" not "exact". Expected to flip top.matchType to "exact" after step 4a (build_alias_table.py merges victim phrases into the bundle table).

G2 — no dedicated 'lemon cakes' phrase key in the alias table; fuzzy fallback returns low-confidence unrelated foods (best score 0.55) rather than surfacing lemon-cake as the clear top hit. Expected to improve after step 4b (variant/alias generation) and to become reachable regardless via step 5's content search over quote text.

G10 — bare single-token query with no exact alias-map key; every candidate ties at fuzzy score 1.0 (full token-overlap + slug bonus cap), so the whole-graph prominence tie-break decides, and tyrion-lannister (higher prominence: 462) outranks the intended tywin-lannister (218). Mechanism = no candidate-length penalty. Expected to be de-biased in step 4c (candidate-length normalization ported to both resolve.py and resolve.ts).
restates the existing resolve_test.ts case as a shared golden case

restates resolve_test.ts's prominence-tiebreak case: a shared phrase resolves to several exact slugs (both score 1.0); the content-rich node (16 quotes) must outrank the empty bare-name bucket (0 quotes).
